subject: Geology
title: Streak and Cleavage
description: A short lesson to help you apply Streak and Cleavage.

hook.text: Hematite looks silver but leaves a red streak on porcelain. The powder color reveals the truth the surface hides.

buildup.text: Streak is the color of a mineral's powder, tested by rubbing it on an unglazed porcelain plate.

discovery.text: Cleavage is how a mineral breaks along flat planes. Mica peels in sheets; calcite splits into rhombs.

twist.text: Fracture is different from cleavage. Quartz has no cleavage, so it breaks with rough, curved surfaces.

climax.text: Streak and cleavage together narrow identification quickly. They reveal internal structure the eye can't see.

punchline.text: Break it or streak it to know the truth.

quiz.question: What is mineral streak?
options: The color of a mineral's surface
The color of a mineral's powder on porcelain
The pattern of cracks in a mineral
